text:
ASAT — Aditya Scholastic Aptitude Test 2026
### ASAT Round 1
- Registration Opens: January 15, 2026
- Registration Closes: February 28, 2026
- ASAT Exam Date: First week of March 2026
- Results Declared: Within 48 hours of exam
- Scholarship Offer Letters: Sent within 3 days of result

### ASAT Round 2
- Registration Opens: March 15, 2026
- Registration Closes: April 30, 2026
- ASAT Exam Date: Second week of May 2026
- Results Declared: Within 48 hours of exam
- Note: Students can attempt ASAT maximum 2 times — higher score considered

### ASAT Round 3 (Final Round)
- Registration Opens: May 15, 2026
- Registration Closes: June 15, 2026
- ASAT Exam Date: Third week of June 2026
- Results Declared: Within 48 hours of exam

AP EAPCET 2026 (External — AP State Exam)
- Application Dates: February 2026 to March 2026 (expected)
- Exam Dates: May 2026 (expected — as per APSCHE schedule)
- Results: June 2026 (expected)
- Counselling by APSCHE: June to July 2026 (expected)
- Note: Aditya University accepts EAPCET ranks for B.Tech and B.Pharmacy admissions

JEE Main 2026 (External — National Exam)
- Session 1 Exam: January 2026 (expected)
- Session 2 Exam: April 2026 (expected)
- Results: Within 2 weeks of exam (expected)
- Note: Aditya University accepts JEE Main scores for B.Tech admissions

NEET 2026 (External — National Exam)
- Exam Date: May 2026 (expected — as per NTA schedule)
- Results: June 2026 (expected)
- Note: Aditya University accepts NEET scores for B.Pharmacy and Pharma-D admissions

ECET 2026 — Lateral Entry (External — AP State Exam)
- Application: March to April 2026 (expected)
- Exam Date: May 2026 (expected)
- Results: June 2026 (expected)
- Note: For B.Tech Lateral Entry (II Year) admissions at Aditya University

metadata.head: ECET 2026 — Lateral Entry (External — AP State Exam)

text:
B.Tech Admissions 2026-27
### Via EAPCET / JEE Rank
- Application at Aditya Opens: June 1, 2026
- Application Closes: July 15, 2026
- Merit List / Call Letters: July 20, 2026 onwards (rolling basis)
- Reporting and Admission: July 21 to August 10, 2026
- Last Date for Admission: August 10, 2026

### Via ASAT
- Applications accepted throughout ASAT rounds (January to June 2026)
- Admission can be completed immediately after ASAT result
- Last Date for ASAT-based Admission: August 10, 2026

### Lateral Entry B.Tech (II Year)
- Application Opens: June 1, 2026
- Application Closes: July 20, 2026
- Reporting and Admission: July 25 to August 10, 2026

- Personal Interview: Conducted on rolling basis after application
- Reporting and Admission: June to August 15, 2026

- Application Opens: May 1, 2026
- Application Closes: July 31, 2026

- Reporting and Admission: July 15 to August 15, 2026

### ARET July 2026 Session

- Personal Interview: Within 2 weeks of result

text:
Academic Calendar 2026-27 (Key Dates)
- Orientation and Freshers Induction: First week of August 2026
- Odd Semester (Sem 1 / 3 / 5 / 7) Begins: August 11, 2026
- Mid Semester Exams (Odd): October 2026
- Odd Semester End Exams: November to December 2026
- Even Semester (Sem 2 / 4 / 6 / 8) Begins: January 2027
- Mid Semester Exams (Even): March 2027
- Even Semester End Exams: April to May 2027

Fee Payment Deadlines 2026-27
- Admission Fee (₹15,000): At time of joining — before seat is confirmed
- First Semester Tuition Fee: At time of joining
- Second Semester Tuition Fee: January 10, 2027
- Hostel Fee (Annual): At time of hostel allotment
- Late Fee Penalty: ₹100 per day after due date

Quick Reference — Last Dates Summary
- Last date B.Tech Admission: August 10, 2026
- Last date BBA / BCA / B.Sc Admission: August 10, 2026
- Last date B.Pharmacy / Pharma-D Admission: August 10, 2026
- Last date MBA Admission: August 15, 2026
- Last date M.Tech / MCA / M.Pharmacy / M.Sc Admission: August 15, 2026
- Last date PhD Admission (July session): August 2026

- Minimum Marks: 60% aggregate in PCM (55% for reserved categories SC/ST/OBC)
- Entrance Accepted: EAPCET (AP & TS) / JEE Main / ASAT
- Age Limit: Minimum 17 years as on December 31, 2026

text:
B.Tech Lateral Entry — Eligibility
- Qualification: Diploma in relevant Engineering branch from a recognised polytechnic
- Minimum Marks: 60% aggregate in Diploma (55% for SC/ST/OBC)
- Entrance Accepted: ECET 2026 (AP & TS)
- Entry: Direct admission into II Year (2nd Year) B.Tech
- Note: Same branch diploma — Branch Rank considered. Different branch — Integrated Rank considered

BCA — Eligibility

metadata.head: B.Sc Cyber Security and Digital Forensics — Eligibility

text:
MBA All Specializations — Eligibility
- Qualification: Any Bachelor's degree (minimum 3 years) from a recognised university
- Minimum Marks: 50% aggregate in graduation
- Entrance Accepted: ICET (AP) / CAT / MAT / NMAT / CMAT / XMAT / ASAT
- Work experience preferred but not mandatory

MBA for Working Professionals — Eligibility
- Qualification: Any Bachelor's degree from a recognised university
- Minimum Marks: 50% aggregate in graduation
- Work Experience: Minimum 1 year of work experience preferred
- Entrance Accepted: ICET / CAT / MAT / ASAT

M.Tech All Branches — Eligibility
- Qualification: B.Tech / B.E in relevant branch with minimum 50% marks
- Entrance Accepted: PGECET (AP) / GATE / ASAT
- GATE Benefit: GATE qualified students eligible for AICTE stipend ₹12,400 per month

MCA — Eligibility
- Qualification: BCA / B.Sc Computer Science / B.Tech (any branch) with minimum 50% marks
- Mathematics at 10+2 or graduation level is mandatory
- Entrance Accepted: ICET (AP) / ASAT / Merit Based

M.Pharmacy All Specializations — Eligibility
- Qualification: B.Pharmacy from a PCI recognised institution with minimum 55% marks
- Entrance Accepted: GPAT / PGECET (Pharmacy) / ASAT

M.Sc Forensic Science — Eligibility
- Qualification: B.Sc in Forensic Science or related Science stream with minimum 50% marks
- Entrance Accepted: PGCET / ASAT / Merit Based

M.Sc Cyber Security and Digital Forensics — Eligibility
- Qualification: B.Sc / B.Tech in CS / IT / Cyber Security with minimum 50% marks
- Entrance Accepted: PGCET / ASAT / Merit Based

metadata.head: M.Sc Cyber Security and Digital Forensics — Eligibility

text:
PhD All Departments — Eligibility
- Qualification: Master's degree in relevant field with minimum 55% marks
- Entrance: Aditya Research Entrance Test (ARET) + Personal Interview
- Departments: CSE, AI&DS, ECE, EEE, Mechanical, Civil, Management, Pharmacy, Forensic Science
- Mode: Full Time (3-5 years) / Part Time (4-6 years)

ASAT — Aditya Scholastic Aptitude Test
- Conducted by: Aditya University
- Fee: ₹500 per attempt (Maximum 2 attempts allowed)
- Purpose: Admission + Scholarship eligibility
- Covers: Aptitude, Reasoning, English, subject-specific questions
- Mode: Online — can be taken from home
- Register at: admissions.adityauniversity.in

Route 1 — Admission via EAPCET / JEE / NEET Rank
### Step 1: Appear for Entrance Exam
- AP EAPCET: Conducted by APSCHE for AP and Telangana students
- JEE Main: Conducted by NTA for students across India
- NEET: For B.Pharmacy and Pharma-D admissions

### Step 2: Apply to Aditya University
- Visit: admissions.adityauniversity.in
- Fill the online application form
- Upload documents: 10th marksheet, 12th marksheet, entrance rank card, Aadhaar, passport photo
- Application fee: Nil (Free application)

### Step 3: Receive Admission Call Letter
- Based on rank and seat availability, Aditya University sends a call letter via email and SMS
- Call letter includes: Reporting date, time, documents checklist, fee payment details

### Step 4: Report to Campus
- Report on the given date with all original documents
- Document verification by Admissions Committee
- Pay ₹15,000 Admission Fee (Non-Refundable) + first semester tuition fee
- Collect admission confirmation letter and student ID

### Step 5: Scholarship Processing
- Scholarship calculated at time of admission based on rank or ASAT score
- Scholarship amount adjusted in fee payment challan
- Submit scholarship documents (if applying for category or sports scholarship)

metadata.head: Route 1 — Admission via EAPCET / JEE / NEET Rank

text:
Route 2 — Admission via ASAT (Aditya Scholastic Aptitude Test)
### Step 1: Register for ASAT
- Register at: admissions.adityauniversity.in
- ASAT Fee: ₹500 per attempt (Maximum 2 attempts)
- ASAT is conducted online — can be taken from home on your own device
- Test covers: Aptitude, Reasoning, English, Mathematics or Science (based on program)

### Step 2: Appear for ASAT
- Receive hall ticket on registered email 2 days before exam
- Take test on scheduled date and time
- Results declared within 48 hours of test

### Step 3: Receive Merit Scholarship Offer
- Based on ASAT score, scholarship offer sent via email
- Scholarship slabs: 10% to 75% of tuition fee based on score
- Accept the offer and confirm admission intent online

### Step 4: Report to Campus and Complete Admission
- Report on given date with original documents
- Document verification, fee payment, and admission confirmation
- Collect student ID and admission letter

metadata.head: Route 2 — Admission via ASAT (Aditya Scholastic Aptitude Test)

text:
Route 3 — Direct Merit Admission (BBA, B.Sc, BCA, MBA, M.Sc)
- Applicable for programs where ASAT or government entrance is optional
- Apply online at admissions.adityauniversity.in
- Shortlisting based on 10+2 or graduation percentage
- Personal interview or counselling call by Admissions Team
- Report to campus on given date for document verification and fee payment

metadata.head: Route 3 — Direct Merit Admission (BBA, B.Sc, BCA, MBA, M.Sc)

text:
Documents Required at Time of Admission (All Programs)
- 10th Class Marksheet and Certificate (original + 2 photocopies)
- 12th Class / Intermediate Marksheet and Certificate (original + 2 photocopies)
- Entrance Exam Rank Card — EAPCET / JEE / NEET / ASAT (original + 2 photocopies)
- Transfer Certificate (TC) from previous institution (original)
- Migration Certificate (for students from outside AP and TS)
- Conduct Certificate from previous institution
- Aadhaar Card (original + 2 photocopies)
- Passport size photographs — 6 copies
- Caste Certificate — if applicable (SC / ST / OBC / EWS)
- Income Certificate — if applying for category scholarship
- Medical Fitness Certificate from a registered doctor
- Anti-Ragging Affidavit — signed by student and parent (format available at admissions office)

metadata.head: Documents Required at Time of Admission (All Programs)

text:
Lateral Entry B.Tech — Special Process
- Eligible: Diploma holders applying for direct II Year B.Tech admission
- Entrance: ECET 2026 (AP or TS)
- Apply at: admissions.adityauniversity.in (Lateral Entry section)
- Additional Document: Diploma marksheets (all semesters) and provisional certificate
- Scholarship up to 100% based on ECET 2026 rank (Rank 1-25 gets 100%)

NRI and International Student Admission Process
- Apply via: admissions.adityauniversity.in (NRI / International section)
- Documents: Passport, Visa, Equivalency certificate for foreign qualifications
- Fee: NRI fee structure applicable — contact admissions office for details
- Contact: Dr. A. Rama Krishna — Cell: 76590 76662

metadata.head: NRI and International Student Admission Process
